workflow_rationale: This is a multi-component feature adding a new onboarding system. Can be built incrementally: (1) state management and detection, (2) welcome modal, (3) tutorial system, (4) sample goals, (5) integration. Each phase can be tested independently.

phases.name: Setup & State Management
phases.description: Create onboarding store, types, and localStorage persistence for tracking first-run and tutorial progress

subtasks.description: Create onboarding Zustand store with first-run detection and tutorial step tracking

verification.expected: Build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

subtasks.notes: Created onboarding.ts Zustand store following patterns from wizard.ts and ui.ts. Implemented first-run detection with localStorage persistence, tutorial step tracking (welcome → create-project → configure-team → start-sprint → view-results → complete), tooltip management, and all required methods (startTutorial, exitTutorial, setStep, completeStep, markFirstRunComplete, dismissTooltip, enableTooltips, disableTooltips, resetOnboarding). Code follows TypeScript conventions and Zustand patterns. Note: npm build verification could not be run in current environment (npm not available), but code structure and syntax verified manually against pattern files.

subtasks.description: Create sample goal data structure for 'Hello World API' demo

verification.expected: Build succeeds, sample goal exports valid data

subtasks.notes: Created frontend/src/lib/sample-goals.ts with comprehensive sample data for 'Hello World API' demo. Includes: SAMPLE_PROJECT (ProjectCreate), SAMPLE_WIZARD_STEP1 (WizardStep1Data), SAMPLE_SPRINT (SprintCreate), SAMPLE_GOAL_TREE (task hierarchy with 4 main tasks and 2 subtasks), SAMPLE_TASKS (full task details with acceptance criteria, results, and reviews), and helper functions isSampleGoal() and getSampleTask(). All TypeScript types correctly imported from types.ts and schemas.ts. Verified with tsc --noEmit - no errors in sample-goals.ts. Code follows patterns from wizard.ts and api.ts. Note: Full build has pre-existing errors in SprintBoard.tsx, TaskCard.tsx, and vite.config.ts (unrelated to this subtask), but sample-goals.ts is verified error-free.

phases.name: Welcome Modal & First-Run Detection
phases.description: Build welcome modal that appears on first launch, explains FoundrAI, and offers guided tour

subtasks.description: Create WelcomeModal component with FoundrAI introduction and 'Start Tutorial' CTA

checks: Modal has close and start tutorial buttons

subtasks.notes: Created WelcomeModal component in frontend/src/components/onboarding/WelcomeModal.tsx following patterns from AddApiKeyModal.tsx. Implemented: modal backdrop with click-to-close, close button (X icon), 'Skip for now' button, 'Start Tutorial' CTA button with Rocket icon, FoundrAI introduction and tagline, 'What you'll learn' section with bullet points, proper accessibility attributes (role, aria-label, aria-modal), focus trap using useFocusTrap hook, and Escape key handling. Also created comprehensive test suite in __tests__/WelcomeModal.test.tsx with 14 test cases covering rendering, user interactions, accessibility, and all button behaviors. Component follows TypeScript conventions and styling patterns (bg-card, border-border, text-foreground, etc.) from existing codebase.

subtasks.description: Integrate WelcomeModal into AppShell to show on first run

checks: Modal doesn't show on subsequent visits

subtasks.notes: Integrated WelcomeModal into AppShell. Shows when isFirstRun is true (fresh localStorage), calls markFirstRunComplete on close, and startTutorial when user clicks Start Tutorial.

phases.name: Interactive Tutorial System
phases.description: Build tutorial overlay with contextual tooltips that guide users through: create project → configure team → start sprint → view results

subtasks.description: Create reusable Tooltip component for contextual help
subtasks.notes: Created reusable Tooltip component with top/bottom/left/right positioning, arrow indicator, hover/focus triggers, configurable delay, and dark mode support.

subtasks.description: Create TutorialOverlay component that highlights UI elements and shows step-by-step guidance
subtasks.notes: Created TutorialOverlay component with highlight cutout via data-tutorial selectors, step-by-step navigation (next/previous/skip), progress bar, Escape key support, and responsive positioning. No TypeScript errors.

subtasks.description: Integrate tutorial steps into Dashboard, New Project, and Sprint views
subtasks.notes: Added data-tutorial attributes to Dashboard (create-project on New Project button), New Project page (configure-team wrapper around ProjectWizard), and Sprint view (start-sprint on SprintLauncher, view-results on CommandCenter).

phases.name: Sample Goal & Quick Start
phases.description: Pre-populate a sample 'Hello World API' goal for first-time users to demonstrate the full workflow

subtasks.description: Create SampleGoalDialog component that offers to use demo goal or create custom
subtasks.notes: Created SampleGoalDialog component offering sample "Hello World API" project or custom start. Follows AddApiKeyModal patterns: focus trap, Escape key, backdrop click, aria attributes. Uses SAMPLE_WIZARD_STEP1 data to pre-fill wizard step 1 via onUseSampleGoal callback.

subtasks.description: Integrate sample goal into project creation flow during tutorial

checks: User can still create custom goal
Sample goal has valid name and description

subtasks.notes: Integrated sample goal auto-fill into WizardStep1 when tutorial is active. Added applySampleGoal action to wizard store. Shows info banner during tutorial mode.

phases.name: Progress Indicator & Completion
phases.description: Show onboarding completion progress and allow tutorial replay from settings

subtasks.description: Create ProgressIndicator component showing onboarding checklist
subtasks.notes: Created ProgressIndicator component showing onboarding checklist with step completion checkmarks, progress bar, current step indicator. Uses onboarding store for state persistence via localStorage. Hidden when no steps completed and tutorial inactive, or when onboarding is fully complete.

subtasks.description: Add 'Replay Tutorial' button to settings page
subtasks.notes: Added Replay Tutorial button to settings page general tab. Button resets onboarding state, starts tutorial, and navigates to home.

phases.name: Integration & E2E Verification
phases.description: Wire all components together, verify complete onboarding flow from first launch to completed sprint

steps: Follow tutorial through: Dashboard → Create Project → Configure Team → Start Sprint
Verify tutorial can be replayed from settings

subtasks.notes: Integrated TutorialOverlay and ProgressIndicator into AppShell (were missing). ProgressIndicator positioned fixed bottom-left. Created comprehensive E2E test suite (OnboardingFlow.test.tsx) covering: first-run detection, tutorial start, step progression through all 5 steps, skip via button and Escape key, progress indicator updates, tutorial replay, localStorage persistence, sample goal data, and complete lifecycle flow. All onboarding components now properly wired together.

acceptance_criteria: First-time users see welcome modal on launch
Tutorial guides through complete project creation flow
Sample goal 'Hello World API' pre-fills wizard
Contextual tooltips appear on first encounter
Tutorial can be replayed from settings

verification_steps.name: Frontend Build
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Build succeeds with no TypeScript errors

verification_steps.name: Browser Verification
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All acceptance criteria verified in browser

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk UX feature - primarily frontend changes with localStorage persistence. Requires browser testing but no backend integration or security concerns.

recoveryNote: Task recovered from stuck state at 2026-03-21T08:04:13.444Z
description:
# Guided Non-Technical Onboarding Experience

Create an interactive first-run experience that guides new users from installation through their first successful sprint completion. Include an interactive tutorial, contextual tooltips, a sample goal that demonstrates the full workflow, and progressive disclosure of advanced features. Target zero-terminal-required for the entire flow.

## Rationale
CrewAI requires significant Python expertise (pain-1-7), and no multi-agent framework has a non-technical-friendly onboarding (gap-7). FoundrAI's web dashboard and project wizard already lower the bar — a guided onboarding experience completes the vision of making AI agent orchestration accessible to non-developers.

## User Stories
- As a non-technical founder, I want a guided walkthrough so that I can use FoundrAI without learning command-line tools
- As a new user, I want to see FoundrAI working with a sample project before configuring my own so that I understand the workflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] First-time users see an interactive welcome flow on first launch
- [ ] Tutorial walks through: create project → configure team → start sprint → view results
- [ ] Sample goal ('Build a Hello World API') pre-populated for first sprint
- [ ] Contextual tooltips explain UI elements on first encounter
- [ ] Progress indicator shows onboarding completion status
- [ ] Tutorial can be replayed from settings
- [ ] No terminal commands required for the entire onboarding flow
- [ ] Time to first sprint < 5 minutes from application launch
